name: Joi
what_it_does: Powerful server-side JavaScript object schema validation library from the hapi.js ecosystem, with Joi.object().keys(), alternatives, and custom validators.
use_cases: Validate incoming HTTP request payloads and query parameters in hapi.js or Express routes using Joi.object().keys() schemas
Define alternative valid shapes for a field with Joi.alternatives().try() to accept multiple data formats in agent inputs
Strip undeclared fields from inputs using { stripUnknown: true } to sanitize agent-received payloads before processing
Write custom validation logic with .custom() validators that receive the value and helpers object for complex business rules
Validate environment configuration objects at application startup to fail fast if required variables are missing or malformed
not_for: Frontend/browser form validation where bundle size matters — Joi is designed for server-side use and is large for client bundles
TypeScript projects requiring static type inference from schema — Joi's TypeScript support is weaker than Zod's
Projects outside the hapi.js ecosystem where lighter alternatives (Zod, Yup) offer better ergonomics with less overhead
best_when: Validating server-side data in Node.js applications, especially within the hapi.js ecosystem, where rich schema composition and alternatives are needed.
avoid_when: You are building a TypeScript-first application where compile-time type safety derived from schemas is a design requirement.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError includes details array with path, type, and message per violation — machine-parseable, though error codes are less standardized than Zod's.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure validation function — deterministic output for any given input with no side effects.
known_agent_gotchas: abortEarly defaults to true — agents must pass { abortEarly: false } to schema.validate() to receive all errors, not just the first
Joi.object() unknown keys are allowed by default; use .unknown(false) or { allowUnknown: false } to reject extra fields strictly
Joi.alternatives().try() matches the first passing schema and discards the rest — ordering of alternatives affects which shape is returned
Custom .custom() validators must call helpers.error() rather than throwing — throwing inside a custom validator produces an uncaught exception
Joi schemas are immutable — chaining methods returns a new schema object; agents that cache a base schema and mutate it will find their changes discarded

security_score_components.security_notes: Server-side library with no network access; ensure ValidationError details are not leaked in API responses, as they describe internal schema structure.
